subject: TSL Weekly Digest — Week of Mar 9, 2026
html:
TSL Internal

TSL Weekly Digest

Week of Mar 9–13, 2026  ·  For Sam & David

What Got Done This Week

Product: New home page shipped — multi-signal feed, significant implementation. Reference check packages feature added (6 references/fund, ~$2,500 value). Featured funds now use weighted scoring. Community page redesigned with vertical layout. 300 entities researched and updated.

Business: TSL Launch Party in SF — 13 LP/allocator attendees. Charlotte sync secured key product direction input (Vantager post-mortem, DocSend extraction path, Burgiss data access). Cendana work session (today). Stifel meeting. Ann meeting.

Platform Pulse

No platform metrics (users, signups, activity) came up in meetings this week. Worth tracking weekly: active user count, new signups, funds researched, community notes added, reference packages sold. Consider adding a 2-min metrics check to the Monday sync with David.

Strategic Insights

The Vantager acquisition by MSCI is the most important competitive signal of the week. Charlotte's breakdown of what made it best-in-class — full auditability to source docs, data room API ingestion, end-deliverable memos, seamless GP permissioning — is essentially a product roadmap handed to you. The $7K payout suggests MSCI bought the team and customer list, not the technology. That means the product gap is still open.

Charlotte's KPI to automate LP workflows by June creates a real near-term conversion opportunity. She's already engaged, has Burgiss data access, and is advising on product direction. The pitch deck upload demo (due ~Mar 18) is the right hook — DocSend extraction is the make-or-break technical piece to solve this weekend.

The SF dinner format worked. 13 LPs in a room with fun-fact poker cards is more memorable than another panel. The NYC event in late April should go bigger on attendees and lean into TSL as a community, not just a tool. Charlotte should co-curate the invite list — her network adds credibility.

Competitive & Market Signals

  • Vantager → MSCI: Acquired. Charlotte frustrated with terms. Product gap still open.
  • Velvet (acquired Revere): Charlotte rolling off as advisor (Vantager conflict). Founder unfocused, eng iteration too slow.
  • Unlimited AI, Cannon Project: Both pre-launch or early. Worth monitoring.
  • Erasmus: CTO/domain expert tension causing product bias toward pure tech. Benchmark-backed but drifting.

Open Items

Owner Item Status
SamPitch deck upload demo⚠️ Due ~Mar 18
SamDocSend extractionBlocking demo
SamCharlotte platform loginUnconfirmed
SamIntro Charlotte to Ben & WillPending
DavidInbound follow-ups (SF week)Carrying forward
BothNYC event — April dates + inviteesCharlotte input needed

Priorities for Next Week

Product

  1. DocSend extraction + pitch deck upload demo — non-negotiable by Mar 18
  2. GP integration design — auditability + permissioning (Vantager's moat, now available)
  3. Bulk fund upload for customers (from DZ Weekly)

GTM

  1. Demo to Charlotte — confirm Burgiss data access, discuss June KPI workflow
  2. NYC event planning — target late April, Charlotte co-curates invites
  3. (David) SF follow-ups — prioritize LPs from dinner with check-writing mandates

Ops

  1. Start tracking platform metrics weekly — user count, funds researched, notes added
  2. Email Cameron re: fund investment (Sam)

Momentum Check

Good week — strong product output and a high-quality LP event. The risk going into next week is the Mar 18 demo deadline with DocSend still unsolved. That's the one thing that could slip. Platform metrics are a blind spot: you're shipping and meeting LPs but not measuring adoption, which makes it hard to know what's actually landing.

Source: Charlotte / Sam Sync (Mar 11), Sam Update #TSLinternal (Mar 11), TSL/Allocator Ideas Brief (Mar 9), Sam / DZ Weekly Catch Up (Mar 9)
